title: Podman cannot update the manifest schema v1 to v2 automatically
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
There is an image with schema v1: `"ManifestType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v1+prettyjws"`. But, after the image mirroring, the digest of this image will be changed, so I want to update the schema version to `v2`.
I follow this [Update to image manifest version 2, schema 2](https://docs.docker.com/registry/spec/deprecated-schema-v1/#update-to-image-manifest-version-2-schema-2).

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1, podman `pull/push` this scehma v1 image.
```yaml
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env jian]$ podman push quay.io/openshifttest/etcd-operator:v0.9.4
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 6716b5119fb8 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 9634398ecc32 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 96b4c9fdee15 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob b9a4a96cf659 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob e79522dce35e skipped: already exists  
Copying blob e7028e7164e7 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env jian]$ podman pull quay.io/openshifttest/etcd-operator:v0.9.4
Trying to pull quay.io/openshifttest/etcd-operator:v0.9.4...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 80b1b554e5b4 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 59265c40e257 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 678f2cfebea6 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 6d4b4c91b3ff skipped: already exists  
Copying blob e05d0d7eb99a skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 8ea0315241b4 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 done  
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
9d5c51d92fbddcda022478def5889a9ceb074305d83f2336cfc228827a03d5d5
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Check the schema version. But, the `ManifestType` still is `v1`.
```yaml
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env jian]$ podman inspect quay.io/openshifttest/etcd-operator:v0.9.4
[
    {
...
        "Annotations": {},
        "ManifestType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v1+prettyjws",
        "User": "etcd-operator",
        "History": [
...
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
It should be changed to `"ManifestType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json"` automatically.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I tried the `pull/push` with `docker` command, it works as expected: the schema v1 was changed to v2 automatically.
```
[jzhang@dhcp-140-36 ~]$ docker version
Client: Docker Engine - Community
 Version:           19.03.0-rc3
 API version:       1.40
 Go version:        go1.12.5
 Git commit:        27fcb77
 Built:             Thu Jun 20 02:02:49 2019
 OS/Arch:           linux/amd64
 Experimental:      false

Server: Docker Engine - Community
 Engine:
  Version:          19.03.0-rc3
  API version:      1.40 (minimum version 1.12)
  Go version:       go1.12.5
  Git commit:       27fcb77
  Built:            Thu Jun 20 02:01:21 2019
  OS/Arch:          linux/amd64
  Experimental:     false
 containerd:
  Version:          1.2.6
  GitCommit:        894b81a4b802e4eb2a91d1ce216b8817763c29fb
 runc:
  Version:          1.0.0-rc8
  GitCommit:        425e105d5a03fabd737a126ad93d62a9eeede87f
 docker-init:
  Version:          0.18.0
  GitCommit:        fec3683
```
1, pull this image with docker 
```yaml
[jzhang@dhcp-140-36 ~]$ docker pull quay.io/openshifttest/etcd-operator:v0.9.4
v0.9.4: Pulling from openshifttest/etcd-operator
[DEPRECATION NOTICE] registry v2 schema1 support will be removed in an upcoming release. Please contact admins of the quay.io registry NOW to avoid future disruption.

8ea0315241b4: Already exists

Digest: sha256:b062d1729b9d99cbda3c2f1d8dd58b54b1ee9df3f02b3410bcb3e5379f1a489b
Status: Downloaded newer image for quay.io/openshifttest/etcd-operator:v0.9.4
quay.io/openshifttest/etcd-operator:v0.9.4
```
2, push this image with docker.
```yaml
[jzhang@dhcp-140-36 ~]$ docker push quay.io/openshifttest/etcd-operator:v0.9.4
The push refers to repository [quay.io/openshifttest/etcd-operator]
b9a4a96cf659: Layer already exists 
6716b5119fb8: Layer already exists 
e7028e7164e7: Layer already exists 
96b4c9fdee15: Layer already exists 
9634398ecc32: Layer already exists 
e79522dce35e: Layer already exists 
v0.9.4: digest: sha256:2cce9f8e95c9b4ce19b9ffbb95298b9ba3c4960dc962030d8bce655f3811adb0 size: 1582
```
3, check the manifest type of this image. The schema v1 was changed to v2 successfully.
```yaml
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env jian]$ podman pull quay.io/openshifttest/etcd-operator:v0.9.4
Trying to pull quay.io/openshifttest/etcd-operator:v0.9.4...
...
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env jian]$ podman inspect quay.io/openshifttest/etcd-operator:v0.9.4
[
    {
        "Id": "9d5c51d92fbddcda022478def5889a9ceb074305d83f2336cfc228827a03d5d5",
...
"ManifestType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json",
....
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env jian]$ podman version
Version:      3.0.2-dev
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.13
Built:        Tue Jun  8 03:52:06 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: First question in my mind is whether Docker is doing the conversion, or the registry is. Can you try with docker.io instead of quay.io?

> First question in my mind is whether Docker is doing the conversion, or the registry is.

I guess the `docker` does the conversion. As you can see from the above steps, I used the same quay.io, the `docker` tool convert it successfully, but `podman` does not.

If the manifest isn't being written in v2s2 format during the `push`, and the image was built locally (i.e., its manifest wasn't pulled from a registry in v2s1format), then it's possible that there was a problem pushing the image's manifest in the v2s2 or OCI format; re-pushing with `--log-level=debug` should tell us more.
The package version is really handy for running down the exact versions of the libraries involved, to see if there's a problem in one of them that's known to have been fixed in a later version.  If you have that information, please be sure to provide it.

(Without debug logs and details, at a first glance) this looks like a currently expected behavior: c/image does not do format conversions unless necessary, and they are not necessary here: `pull` to c/storage can use schema1, and push to a registry can use schema1.

IIRC `docker push` automatically converts to schema2 because `docker pull` does not actually record the full manifest, so it _always_ tries schema2 first; it’s somewhat of an implementation accident that pull/push does a conversion.

With Podman, users have explicit control, so `podman push --format v2s2` (or `podman push --format {oci,v2s1}`) allows specifying the wanted format directly.

---

If all you want to do is to convert an on-registry image to a different format, and you don’t need it pulled locally, it’s _much_ more efficient to use `skopeo copy --format v2s2 docker://$source docker://$destination`, which works on tarball streams and does not need to extract the image into a set of hundreds/thousands of small local files (with all the FS usage and disk seeks and journal/cache flushes that implies) only to read the local files again.

---

I don’t have a strong opinion on whether Podman should be compatible with Docker in this regard; I’m leaning towards “no, this is mostly a Docker implementation accident, and we have better tools with better user control already”, but the increasing presence of schema1-rejecting registries, along with https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/272 , might make defaulting to schema2 for schema1 sources more attractive. (The c/image/copy code would need to be taught about this, because we probably don’t want to hard-code v2s2-_only_ , but something like “try (v2s2, v2s1, OCI) in this order”.)
Got it, many thanks for your clarfication!
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I believe this is solved, closing.  Reopen if I am mistaken.
